name: wide-blast-radius
description: A change to a shared auth client imported by multiple services — moderate blast radius, warrants review.

pr.title: Refactor token refresh logic in shared auth client
pr.author: marcus
pr.description: Refactors the token refresh flow in the shared auth client to use sliding window expiry instead of fixed TTL. Should be transparent to callers.

agentResponse.summary: The changed auth client is imported by at least three downstream services (api, worker, dashboard). A behavioural shift in token expiry will affect every authenticated call across those boundaries.

findings.title: Touches shared auth client used by many services
findings.detail: authClient.ts is a cross-cutting dependency. Changing token refresh semantics from fixed TTL to sliding window affects every service that calls refreshToken(), including critical auth middleware in packages/api.

findings.title: Three downstream services import this path
findings.detail: packages/api, packages/worker, and apps/dashboard all import refreshToken() from authClient.ts. Any unexpected side-effect in the new sliding-window logic propagates to all of them simultaneously.
